name: Angel Food Summer Pudding
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup orange-flavored liqueur or orange juice
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, mix together berries, sugar, liqueur, and lemon peel.
Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until berries are soft and sugar has dissolved, 5 to 8 minutes. Do not overcook. Let cool.
2. Trim the cake of any browned edges using a serrated knife. Cut the cake into half-inch slices, then cut those slices into triangles.
3. Tightly line the bottom and sides of a 1 1/2 to 2 quart bowl or souffle dish with three-quarters of the cake. If necessary, fill any gaps with small pieces of cake.
4. Spoon the filling into the cake-lined bowl. Top with remaining cake triangles, covering berries completely.
5. Place a plate, just slightly smaller than the bowl's diameter, on top of the pudding. Weigh down the saucer with a 4 or 5 pound can and chill for 8 hours or overnight.
6. Remove the plate and can. Carefully unmold pudding onto a serving plate.
7. To prepare topping, beat cream at medium speed until soft peaks form. Beat in confectioners' sugar.
8. Pipe small mounds of cream mixture on top of pudding using a pastry bag fitted with a large star tip. Garnish with fresh berries and sprigs of fresh mint.
